mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth combined with rising housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion. When INDPRO rises month-over-month while HOUST exceeds 1800 units, equity risk premiums contract and S&P 500 forward returns turn positive over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 15 basis points per month over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0 percent year-over-year, real disposable income becomes the dominant pricing factor. Rising RSAFS under these conditions signals resilient consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in more than 60% of triggered windows when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0 percent and RSAFS rises concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exhibits a persistent positive trend that leads equity valuations. A sequential increase in GDPC1 directly improves earnings guidance, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces a negative direction-adjusted return in more than 50% of observations across a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5 percent, borrowing costs suppress corporate leverage and equity valuations. This threshold breach triggers a negative repricing of future cash flows, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for more than 65% of the time following a FEDFUNDS breach above 4.5 percent over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth amplifies the impact of real disposable income on corporate earnings. When PAYEMS rises month-over-month alongside increasing RSAFS, aggregate demand shocks transmit directly to equity prices, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression of 20-day forward returns over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: During supply-constrained periods where industrial production outpaces labor force participation, profit margins expand due to pricing power. This margin expansion drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days, provided INDPRO growth exceeds PAYEMS growth.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.65) when INDPRO growth consistently outpaces PAYEMS growth over a 2-year window.
